verdict: Paid Instagram is delivering 181 leads at roughly $27 each — well below legal industry norms.
diagnosis: $4,880 in spend produced 181 leads and 1,052 landing page views this period, beating the $67–$254 legal benchmark range on Meta.

signals.headline: 181 leads at $27 each
signals.detail: Cost per lead sits well under legal category averages this period. The Instagram lead campaign is the workhorse driving pipeline.

signals.headline: 10 new five-star reviews added
signals.detail: Google profile gained 10 reviews this period, all five stars, lifting total to 253. Trust signal is strengthening fast.

signals.headline: 123 phone calls from Google
signals.detail: Call clicks from the Google Business Profile show local search demand. Direction requests stayed at zero — phone is the channel.

signals.headline: 1,386 link clicks, 1,052 page views
signals.detail: Roughly 76% of clicks reach the landing page. Drop-off is reasonable but worth tightening for cheaper leads.

signals.headline: Instagram followers stuck at 28
signals.detail: Organic Instagram presence is essentially dormant. Paid is carrying everything — no owned audience to fall back on.

benchmark.label: Benchmark
benchmark.subheader: Legal Meta cost per lead: $21–$254 range
benchmark.body: Industry projections place 2026 law firm Meta cost per lead near $21, with broader legal reports showing $67–$254 depending on practice area. Your $27 sits at the efficient end of that range.

seasonal.label: Seasonal
seasonal.body: Legal advertiser competition on Meta keeps climbing through 2026 as more firms shift budget to paid social. Cost per lead and cost per thousand impressions trend upward heading into Q3.

competitor.label: Competitor
competitor.body: Facebook lead campaigns for attorneys average a 10.53% conversion rate. Firms winning now combine credibility cues — reviews, attorney profiles, case results — with tight intake follow-up under five minutes.

recommendations.title: Test a five-minute lead callback workflow
recommendations.detail: Conversion rates drop sharply when leads wait past five minutes. Consider routing the 181 form fills straight to a dialer or text-back so intake never goes cold.

recommendations.title: Ask recent five-star reviewers for short video clips
recommendations.detail: Ten new five-star reviews landed this period. Worth asking two or three to record a 15-second clip — testimonial creative typically lifts Meta conversion rates.

recommendations.title: Add qualifying questions to the lead form
recommendations.detail: With 181 leads coming in cheap, the next gain is quality. Test one or two screening questions — debt amount, collector type — to filter the pipeline before intake calls.
